url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation. Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page. Each pick's competitor section includes 4-6 suggested queries + 4-6 known specialist-firm-class targets (e.g. Mercer Tax / Cobalt Tax / Saffery Champness ATED desk / BDO ATED / Crowe UK / ETC Tax / Ross Martin Group for B13 amendments + B14 valuation + B16 mistakes + B18 contaminated land; BDO international tax / Smith & Williamson / Saffery international / Crowe UK / Buzzacott / RSM international / Grant Thornton international for B15 AEOI + B17 non-resident-company CT transition).
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §1.C (Bewley uninhabitable-property test Wave 7 SDLT extension lock 2026-05-24) + §10 (DTAs general framing) + §11 + §11.A (RoE / ECTEA 2022 parallel transparency) + §16 (DTAs Wave 2 extension) + §17 (Leaving UK / expat full) + §17.4 (NRCGT TCGA 1992 s.1A + Schs 1A/1B/4AA) + §17.5 (NRL scheme operational mechanics + NRL1/NRL2/NRL3/NRL6) + §17.6 (Domicile reform 2025+ FIG/TRF/LTR) + §18 (Wave 3 ATED extension full) + §18.1 (2026/27 + 2025/26 bands verified gov.uk 2026-05-22) + §18.2 (chargeable persons) + §18.3 (relief catalogue ss.133-150) + §18.4 (return mechanics + 30 April + £100/£200/£300 + 5-yearly revaluation + PRBC + mixed-use) + §18.5 (ATED-CGT abolition FA 2019 + NRCGT interaction) + §18.6 (RoE parallel) + §18.7 (OTM letters campaign) + §18.8 (do-not-write list — the operative drift catalogue) + §21.A (CT 3-figure framework) + §21.5 (CIHC depth + s.18N(3)) + §27 (HMRC enquiry / appeals + Sch 24 / Sch 55 / Sch 56 + Perrin + Martland + 30-day window + statutory review) + §27.2 (F-5 lock Sch 24 careless-unprompted 0% floor NO 12-month qualifier; qualifier exists in Sch 41 para 13 only) + §27.4 (Perrin four-stage verbatim) + §27.5 (Martland three-stage) + §27.9 (do-not-write list verbatim) house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual + BAILII verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. F-5 lock per §27.2 (Sch 24 para 10 careless-unprompted 0% floor has NO 12-month qualifier; qualifier exists in Sch 41 para 13 only) carries verbatim where relevant (most directly engages B13 amendment penalty-mitigation + B15 AEOI WDF disclosure timing + B16 mistakes-orientation Sch 24 considerations). ITTOIA 2005 s.396B TAAR cite-correctness per Wave 6 §16.40 lock not directly bitten in B-B3 picks. Wave 6 §21.4 + §22 + §15.4 enacted-Act drift catches carried (FA 2026 s.4(1)(b) substitution → 35.75% s.455 dividend upper rate; NRB freeze to 5 April 2031; AIM 50% sub-tier separate) — not directly bitten in B-B3 picks. 1 April 2027 next 5-yearly ATED revaluation date per HP §18.4 verified gov.uk 2026-05-22 is LOAD-BEARING for B14 valuation-date page (s.102 + s.102(2A) FA 2015 on-ramp clause: 1 April 2027 revaluation bites 2028/29 NOT 2027/28). FA 2019 Sch 5 commencement 6 April 2020 LOAD-BEARING for B17 non-resident-company CT transition. FA 2017 Sch 18 RTC closed 30 September 2018 + FTC permanent thereafter (200% PLR base + 100% disclosure floor) LOAD-BEARING for B15 AEOI. FA 2015 Sch 21 Cat-2 1.5x + Cat-3 2x territorial uplift LOAD-BEARING for B13 amendments + B15 AEOI + B16 mistakes + B18 contaminated land. EPA 1990 Part 2A + s.78A contaminated-land definition LOAD-BEARING for B18. Bewley v HMRC [2019] UKFTT 65 (TC) HP §1.C cross-reference LOAD-BEARING for B18 ATED-scope-vs-Bewley distinction. SI 2015/878 (CRS + FATCA + DAC2) + SI 2023/817 (DAC7-equivalent platform reporting) LOAD-BEARING for B15 AEOI.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified FA 2013 Sch 33 paras 1 / 3 / 4 / 8 / 21 (B13 amendments); FA 2013 s.102 + s.102(2A) FA 2015 on-ramp + s.103 + s.104 + s.105 (B14 valuation); SI 2015/878 + FA 2017 Sch 18 + FA 2015 Sch 21 architecture (B15 AEOI); FA 2013 Part 3 + ss.94 + 99 + 101 + 102 + 102(2A) + 103 + 116 + 117 + 133-150 + Sch 33 + FA 2019 ATED-CGT abolition + ECTEA 2022 RoE (B16 mistakes-orientation); FA 2019 Sch 5 architecture map (B17 non-resident-company CT transition); FA 2013 s.134 verbatim + s.138 + s.141 + CTA 2009 Part 14 (LRR) + EPA 1990 Part 2A architecture + Bewley v HMRC [2019] UKFTT 65 (TC) (B18 contaminated land). Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §1.C + §10 + §11 + §11.A + §16 + §17 (full) + §17.4 + §17.5 + §17.6 + §18 (full) + §21.A + §21.5 + §27 + §27.2 (F-5 lock) + §27.4 + §27.5 + §27.9 HP locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch were unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (matching M3-B-B1 D-2 + M3-B-B2 D-2 + M3-A-B1 D-2 + M3-A-B2 D-2 + M3-A-B3 D-2 + M3-A-B4 D-2 + M3-C-B1 D-2 + M3-C-B2 D-2 patterns). Each extended brief landed at +271 to +335 lines of additional content (Stage 2 research target list with VERIFIED URLs + worked examples 5-6 per pick + 12-14 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 1 seed work log appendix). This pattern across all M3 batches confirms Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_ATED_DEEP_CLUSTER_DISCIPLINE — picks 1 (B13 amendments) + 2 (B14 valuation) + 4 (B16 mistakes-orientation) + 6 (B18 contaminated land) form a 4-page ATED deep-cluster all anchored on §18 (Wave 3 ATED extension full) + §27 (enquiry / appeals architecture). Cluster-level differentiation discipline maintained at Stage 2: B13 = decision-and-planning page on amendment (period-boundary architecture, 12-months-from-filing-myth refusal, penalty-mitigation tax-tips, relief-restoration, beyond-window routes); B14 = architecture-first orientation on s.102 multi-layered valuation framework (5-yearly Crown dates + substantial-acquisition + partnership / CIS variants + s.102(2A) FA 2015 on-ramp + £40k linked-transaction + valuation→band→daily-amount chain); B16 = prevention-first orientation pivot consolidating HP §18.8 do-not-write list into 12-mistake catalogue with prevention discipline; B18 = ATED-first cross-regime page on contaminated-land remediation (s.134 transitional + s.138 / s.141 trading-stock + LRR cross-reference + EPA 1990 Part 2A + Bewley distinction + NRCGT s.38 interaction). Each pick's anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk + structurally-distinct opening + differentiating worked-example archetypes. RUN session pairing of B13 + B14 + B16 + B18 should hold the line on per-page distinctness; B16 explicitly cross-links to all of B13 + B14 + B18 + the rest of the cluster.

D-4 STAGE2_AEOI_AND_CT_TRANSITION_PAIR — picks 3 (B15 AEOI) + 5 (B17 non-resident-company CT transition) form a 2-page non-resident-landlord pair both anchored on §10 + §16 + §17 + §17.4 + §17.5 + §11 + §11.A HP locks. Cluster-level differentiation: B15 = international information-flow architecture (CRS multilateral + FATCA US bilateral + DAC2 EU legacy + DAC7-equivalent platform reporting + MDR + FA 2017 Sch 18 FTC + FA 2015 Sch 21 Cat-2/Cat-3 + WDF correction route + HMRC Connect cross-match + landlord-specific exposure maps); B17 = corporate-tax structural shift architecture (FA 2019 Sch 5 commencement 6 April 2020 + CTA 2009 s.5 + Part 4 + TIOPA 2010 Part 10 CIR + CTA 2009 Part 5 + s.441 loan relationships + TIOPA 2010 Part 6A hybrid mismatch + CTA 2010 Part 7ZA loss restriction + Part 5 group relief + three-regime picture CT/ATED/NRCGT + RoE parallel trio). Cross-links bidirectional: B15 references B17 for the 2020 CT-scope shift bringing non-resident corporate landlords into LRR / AEOI-corporate-scope; B17 references B15 for the AEOI information-flow architecture that surfaces non-compliance discovered through the new CT regime.

D-5 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 + M3-A-B2 D-5 + M3-B-B1 D-5 + M3-B-B2 D-5 patterns (~2-2.5 hours for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-6 STAGE2_CROSS_BATCH_CONSISTENCY — pick 4 (B16 mistakes-orientation) and pick 6 (B18 contaminated land) both reference back to picks 1 + 2 (B13 amendments + B14 valuation) from this same batch, plus to picks completed in M3-B-B1 + M3-B-B2 prior batches (B4 OTM letters; B5 strategic; B8 + B9 penalty + appeal architecture; B10 rates; B11 clawback; B12 related-persons-decision). The ATED cluster is now substantially complete across M3-B-B1 + M3-B-B2 + M3-B-B3; B16 (this batch) is the orientation pivot that ties them together. RUN session should respect the cluster architecture: B16 forward-links down to deep-dives (B5 strategic / B8 + B9 penalty / B10 rates / B11 clawback / B12 related-persons / B13 amendments / B14 valuation / B18 contaminated land); deep-dives back-link up to B16 for upstream prevention.

D-7 STAGE2_LOAD_BEARING_LOCKS_VERIFIED — the four most-frequently-mis-stated rules in the ATED + non-resident-landlord architecture (relevant across all 6 picks) have been verified verbatim at Stage 1 + cross-checked at Stage 2: (a) FA 2013 Sch 33 para 3(3) period-boundary amendment window (NOT 12-months-from-filing — B13 + B16); (b) FA 2013 s.102(2A) FA 2015 on-ramp clause (1 April 2027 revaluation bites 2028/29 NOT 2027/28 — B14 + B16); (c) FA 2019 ATED-CGT abolition 6 April 2019 (gains now NRCGT not 28% ATED-CGT — B16); (d) FA 2019 Sch 5 commencement 6 April 2020 (non-resident company UK property income moved IT → CT — B17 + cross-applied B15 + B16 + B18). Each is LOAD-BEARING for the corresponding Misframe grep at RUN write time. RUN sessions must reproduce each verbatim from legislation.gov.uk at write per §16.35.

D-8 STAGE2_BEWLEY_CROSS_REGIME_INTEGRATION — pick 6 (B18 contaminated land) integrates the Bewley uninhabitable-property test (HP §1.C Wave 7 SDLT extension lock 2026-05-24) into the ATED scope analysis (Example 5 walks Cregeen Holdings as a Bewley-state property where ATED scope falls away). This is a meaningful cross-bucket integration: Bewley was originally a Wave 7 SDLT lock, B18 demonstrates the doctrinal extension into ATED scope (s.116 dwelling definition question). RUN session must walk this carefully — the SDLT extension is direct authority; the ATED extension is fact-specific but doctrinally available. HP §1.C carries the SDLT-side framing; B18 surfaces the ATED-side reasoning. Potential new HP §29.X candidate flagged for manager triage.

D-9 STAGE2_POLLUTER_EXCLUSION_TRAP_HIGHLIGHTED — pick 6 (B18) Example 4 walks the Ironwood Developments Group scenario where a polluting sibling transfers contaminated land to a redevelopment sibling, exposing the s.146 polluter-exclusion trap (LRR denied because claimant + connected person is the polluter). This is the most expensive LRR mistake in the corporate-group context and is under-walked by existing LRR pages on the site. RUN session must surface this prominently in the B18 page; potential F-numbered drift catch if existing LRR sibling pages omit the connected-persons extension of the polluter exclusion (Stage 2 verifies at write).

D-10 STAGE2_FA_2019_SCH_5_TRANSITION_MAP — pick 5 (B17) Example 1 walks the before/after architecture map as a `<table>` showing 11 elements (charging statute, tax rate, return form, tax registration, NRL withholding, NRL1, CIR, loan relationships, hybrid mismatch, loss restriction, group relief) shifting from pre-2020 IT-regime to post-2020 CT-regime. This map is the canonical orientation artefact for the 2020 transition; potential cross-link target for the existing NRL-scheme cluster pages if those pages do not currently address the post-2020 credit-to-CT pipeline shift (Stage 2 raises F-numbered drift catch candidacy for §16.43 mechanical back-patch sweep). Pre-2020 IT-regime cluster pages on the site should be reviewed; B17 is the corporate-transition specialist + the before/after map is the orientation artefact.
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Quality bar) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: B13 ated-return-amendment (vs existing procedural sibling `ated-return-amendment-corrections-procedure` + 12-months-from-filing-myth refusal anchor); B14 ated-valuation-date (vs existing event-focused sibling `ated-valuation-date-rules-2027-revaluation` + architecture-not-event anchor); B15 automatic-exchange-of-information (vs existing NRL + NRCGT + RoE pages + AEOI-makes-offshore-invisible-untenable anchor); B16 avoiding-common-mistakes-ated (vs existing positive-framing compliance sibling `ated-six-step-compliance-walkthrough-uk-non-natural-persons` + prevention-first negative-framing counterpart anchor); B17 changes-nrl-companies (vs existing generic NRL-scheme orientation `non-resident-landlord-scheme-uk-complete-guide` + 2020 structural-shift before/after architecture anchor); B18 contaminated-land-remediation (vs existing LRR-first deep-dive pages + ATED-first cross-regime anchor + Bewley cross-reference + SPR risk-assessment anchor). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; each worked-example persona surname differs per pick (B13 amendments: Topaz Holdings + Mr Topaz + Susan Hartnell / Cordoba Holdings Cayman / Acer Property Partners LLP / Sapphire Estates Cayman; B14 valuation: Trevor Holdings + Olivetti Holdings + Saffron Estates Partnership LLP + Saffron Holdings B Limited; B15 AEOI: Mr Khoury (Lebanese UAE) + Mrs Olsson (Swedish) + Mr Hayek (UK Airbnb host) + Mrs Henderson (UK-resident US citizen); B16 mistakes-orientation: Trevor Holdings + Mr Trevor + Sarah Quayle / Olivetti Holdings / Saffron Estates Cayman / Ironwood Holdings + Mr Ironwood; B17 NRL-companies: Quayle Property Holdings BVI / Crosby Holdings BVI / Kissack Property LLC + Mr Kissack / Cregeen Estates Cayman; B18 contaminated land: Bridge Holdings + Magnolia Property Investments + Ironwood Developments Group / Cregeen Holdings). Cross-batch persona surname collisions noted: 'Cregeen' surname appears in B17 pick 5 (Cregeen Estates Cayman — non-resident corporate landlord three-regime example) and B18 pick 6 (Cregeen Holdings Limited — UK ltd-co Bewley-state Victorian school building); contexts are sufficiently different (non-resident-corporate three-regime context vs UK-ltd-co Bewley-state contaminated dwelling) that confusion is unlikely; 'Trevor Holdings' appears in B14 pick 2 (Examples 2 + 5 — non-partnership-company £3.4m London town house for s.102 + s.102(2A) on-ramp walks) and B16 pick 4 (Example 2 — UK-ltd-co £1.4m London BTL flat for ATED-overseas-only myth refutation); contexts are sufficiently different (architecture-page valuation-cycle vs orientation-page scope-test) that confusion is unlikely. 'Olivetti Holdings' appears in B14 pick 2 (Example 3 — £40k substantial-acquisition aggregation walk) and B16 pick 4 (Example 3 — claim-only-return discipline walk); contexts sufficiently different (£40k aggregation vs Sch 55 escalator). 'Saffron Estates' appears in B14 (Partnership LLP — partnership variant walk) and B16 (Cayman — ATED-CGT abolition walk); contexts sufficiently different (partnership variant vs ATED-CGT abolition). 'Ironwood' appears in B16 (Holdings Limited + Mr Ironwood — 2027 on-ramp walk) and B18 (Developments Group Industrial + Property — polluter-exclusion trap walk); contexts sufficiently different (timing-myth walk vs polluter-exclusion structural-group walk). RUN session should review for inadvertent surname collisions; the structures are different enough that confusion is unlikely but cross-batch persona discipline could be tightened.
